text: If an effective rent ceiling is eliminated, which of the following is most likely to occur in the rental housing market?

options.A: An increase in the demand for housing, resulting in a decrease in the quantity of housing supplied
options.B: An increase in rents, resulting in an increase in the quantity of housing supplied
options.C: An increase in the demand for housing, resulting in an increase in the quantity of housing demanded
options.D: A decrease in rents, resulting in an increase in the quantity of housing supplied
options.E: A decrease in the demand for housing, resulting in an increase in the quantity of housing supplied any part of this page is illegal.

text: Assume that the market for lemonade is perfectly competitive and currently in equilibrium. Lemons are key ingredients in lemonade. If the price of lemons decreases, how will the lemonade market be affected?

options.A: Supply will shift leftward, increasing the equilibrium price and decreasing the equilibrium quantity of lemonade.
options.B: Supply will shift rightward, increasing the equilibrium price and increasing the equilibrium quantity of lemonade.
options.C: Supply will shift rightward, decreasing the equilibrium price and increasing the equilibrium quantity of lemonade.
options.D: Demand will shift leftward, decreasing the equilibrium price and decreasing the equilibrium quantity of lemonade.
options.E: Demand will shift rightward, increasing the equilibrium price and increasing the equilibrium quantity of lemonade.

options.A: hire more labor and less capital
options.B: hire less labor and more capital
options.C: hire more labor and more capital
options.D: hire less labor and less capital

text: Assume that a profit-maximizing, perfectly competitive firm has economic losses in the short run. If the firm continues to produce and sell its goods, then which of the following must be true?

options.A: The firm is covering all of its fixed and variable costs of production.
options.B: The firm is covering all of its fixed costs but not all of its variable costs of production.
options.C: The firm is covering all of its variable costs but not all of its fixed costs of production.
options.D: The firm is covering all of its implicit costs but not all of its explicit costs.
options.E: The firm must have raised the price of its goods in order to minimize its losses.

options.C: the products produced by different firms are not identical
options.D: it is easy for firms to enter or exit the market
options.E: the marginal cost rises as output produced increases

Questions 25-26 refer to the monopoly graph below, where M C equals marginal cost, A T C equals average total cost, D equals demand, and M R equals marginal revenue. he figure shows a graph with the horizontal axis labeled Quantity and the vertical axis labeled Price. There are 3 points on the horizontal axis labeled, from left to right, Q 1, Q 2, and Q 3.  There are 4 points on the vertical axis labeled, from bottom to top, P 1, P 2, P 3, and P 4.  There are 2 solid straight lines on the graph.  One solid straight line is labeled D and begins at the top of the vertical axis, slopes down to the right, and ends above the horizontal axis.  The second solid straight line is labeled M R and always lies below line D. Line M R begins at the top of the vertical axis where line D starts and slopes down to the right, ending above the horizontal axis between Q 2 and Q 3. There are also 2 curved lines on the graph.  One curve is labeled M C and starts above the horizontal axis to the left of Q 1, curves up to the right, crossing lines M R and D, and ends approximately above a point just to the left of Q 3. Curve M C intersects line M R at a point with coordinates Q 1 and P 1 and intersects line D at a point with coordinates Q 2 and P 3.  The second curve is labeled A T C. It begins between lines M R and D toward the top left of the graph, curves down to a minimum where it intersects with M C at a point with coordinates between Q 1 and Q 2 and between P 1 and P 2, then curves up, crosses D, and ends above a point to the right of Q 3.  Curve ATC intersects line D at a point with coordinates Q 3 and P 2. There is 1 unlabeled point on line D with coordinates Q 1 and P 4.
